text.title: Roadmap Drift Detection Checklist Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Roadmap Drift Detection Checklist. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: ≥70% of completed tickets in the period must be tagged to a roadmap outcome ID.
rule.rationale: ≥70% of completed tickets in the period must be tagged to a roadmap outcome ID.

rule.statement: No 'orphan' high-effort tickets without an outcome tag at sprint close.
rule.rationale: No 'orphan' high-effort tickets without an outcome tag at sprint close.

rule.statement: Candidate solutions older than 30 days without a validation experiment are marked decayed.
rule.rationale: Candidate solutions older than 30 days without a validation experiment are marked decayed.

rule.statement: Confidence labels are refreshed monthly; stale labels are downgraded one level.
rule.rationale: Confidence labels are refreshed monthly; stale labels are downgraded one level.

rule.statement: Every drift item produces a named action with owner + due date.
rule.rationale: Every drift item produces a named action with owner + due date.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-roadmap-drift-detection-checklist.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
